parameterGroups.label: Output
parameterGroups.rationale: Base-class output controls shared by all SoundGenerators

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The output volume as normalised linear gain (0.0 to 1.0), not decibels. Use a SimpleGain effect in the FX chain for decibel-scaled volume control.

parameterGroups.label: Voice Management
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls how voices are allocated and killed across all child sound generators

parameters.name: Kill Fade Time
parameters.description: The fade out time in milliseconds when voices are killed by exceeding the voice limit or by a voice killer

disabledModChains.name: Pitch Modulation
disabledModChains.note: Disabled in base data. Pitch modulation at the container level is not exposed - child synths have their own pitch modulation chains.

gaps.question: How does SynthChain render its children? Does renderNextBlock iterate child synths and sum their outputs, or does it delegate to a different mechanism? What is the mixing order?
gaps.context: Need to understand the container's role: is it a simple mixer of children, or does it do additional processing beyond summing?

gaps.question: The Gain Modulation chain constrainer is 'TimeVariantModulator' - meaning only monophonic modulators are allowed. Is this because the gain is applied to the summed output (post-mix) rather than per-voice?
gaps.context: Important for users to understand that this is a master gain, not a per-voice gain.

gaps.question: The constrainer is '*' meaning any SoundGenerator can be a child. Is there a practical limit on nesting depth (e.g. SynthChain inside SynthChain)?
gaps.context: Users need to know if deep nesting is supported or if there are gotchas.

gaps.question: Where does the FX chain (constrained to MasterEffect) apply in the signal path? After summing all children and applying gain, or between children and gain?
gaps.context: Need to show correct signal flow order in pseudo-code.

gaps.question: Does VoiceLimit apply globally across all children, or per child? When the limit is reached, which voices get killed first?
gaps.context: Critical for users managing polyphony in complex patches.

gaps.question: Is the root SynthChain (master container) treated differently from nested SynthChains? Does it handle the final output routing, sample rate setup, or other global responsibilities?
gaps.context: The root SynthChain is always present in every HISE project - need to clarify if it has special behaviour.

gaps.question: Does SynthChain have a custom editor or FloatingTile content type?

notes: SynthChain is the fundamental container in HISE's module tree. Every HISE project has a root SynthChain ('Master Chain'). It sums the audio output of its child SoundGenerators, applies gain modulation (monophonic only - TimeVariantModulator constrainer), and routes through a MasterEffect FX chain. The Pitch Modulation chain is disabled at the container level. The constrainer '*' means any SoundGenerator type can be added as a child, including nested SynthChains and SynthGroups. SynthChain differs from SynthGroup in that it does not share modulation between children and has no FM or unison capabilities.
